data.title: Vitura Health Director Gerard Fogarty AO Steps Down with Minimal Shareholdings
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Cronos Australia Ltd. ( (AU:VIT) ) is now available.

Vitura Health Limited has announced that director Gerard Fogarty AO has ceased to be a director of the company as of 7 April 2026, with no securities held in his own name at the time of his departure. His indirect interest in the company is limited to 342,428 ordinary shares held by his spouse, Amanda Dorothy Fogarty, and there are no disclosed interests in any related contracts, indicating a relatively clean change in the company’s board composition with limited direct implications for its capital structure.

The notice formalises Fogarty’s exit under ASX listing rule 3.19A.3 and Corporations Act disclosure requirements, underscoring Vitura Health’s adherence to governance and transparency standards in reporting director interests. The change may prompt stakeholders to watch for future board appointments or strategic shifts, but the absence of complex shareholdings or contract ties suggests minimal immediate disruption to governance or shareholder alignment.

Vitura Health Limited is an Australian listed company operating in the healthcare sector. The company’s securities are traded on the ASX, and its governance and director disclosures are reported in accordance with Australian corporate and listing rules.

data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Orezone Gold Corporation Registered Shs ( (AU:ORE) ) has provided an update.

Orezone Gold Corporation has disclosed a change in the holdings of director Joseph Conway, detailing his prior interests in fully paid common shares, multiple tranches of stock options at different strike prices and expiries, and deferred share units. The notice shows that on 27 March 2026 Conway exercised 58,000 options at C$1.05, acquiring an equivalent number of common shares, and subsequently disposed of those options, signaling a routine equity compensation transaction that modestly shifts his mix of derivative-based to direct share ownership.

While the quantum of securities involved is limited relative to the director’s overall holdings, the transaction slightly increases the free float of fully paid common shares and reduces outstanding options at that strike price. For stakeholders, including current and prospective investors, the filing provides incremental transparency into insider activity and confirms ongoing alignment of executive incentives with equity performance, but it does not indicate any broader strategic or operational change at the company.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:ORE) stock is a Buy with a A$4.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Orezone Gold Corporation Registered Shs stock, see the AU:ORE Stock Forecast page.

More about Orezone Gold Corporation Registered Shs

Orezone Gold Corporation is a gold mining company whose primary business is the exploration, development, and operation of gold projects. The company’s securities are listed on the ASX, and its capital structure includes fully paid common shares, stock options with various exercise prices and maturities, and deferred share units, reflecting a typical incentive mix for a mid-tier mining issuer.

An update from Magellan Financial Group Ltd ( (AU:MFG) ) is now available.

Magellan Financial Group has reported the lapse of several classes of conditional equity securities, including matching awards, employee equity grants, long-term incentive plan rights and options expiring in April 2027. In total, more than 330,000 rights and options ceased on 31 March 2026 because performance or vesting conditions were not met or became incapable of being satisfied, resulting in a reduction of potential future equity dilution for existing shareholders.

The cessation affects instruments issued under employee and executive incentive arrangements, signalling that certain long-term and performance-based hurdles were not achieved within the required timeframe. While the announcement does not alter current issued capital, it clarifies that these unvested awards and options will not convert into ordinary shares, refining Magellan’s capital management profile and potentially influencing perceptions of alignment between management remuneration and shareholder outcomes.

data.title: Bowen Coking Coal shareholders face loss of equity under Argo-led restructuring plan
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Bowen Coking Coal Ltd ( (AU:BCB) ) has issued an announcement.

Bowen Coking Coal Ltd and several related entities are operating under a single deed of company arrangement after creditors voted in February 2026 to execute a restructuring proposal put forward by Argo Bowen 2 Pty Ltd. The group, which focuses on coking coal and associated marketing and management services, is under the control of deed administrators Mark Holland and Shaun Fraser as they seek to stabilise the business and address creditor claims.

As part of the restructuring, the deed administrators have applied to the Supreme Court of New South Wales for orders allowing the transfer of 100% of Bowen Coking Coal’s shares and convertible securities to Argo for no consideration, on the basis that the shares are of no value. The court will hold a final hearing on 23 April 2026, and shareholders and creditors have been invited to review detailed explanatory materials and, if they wish to object or be heard, to enter an appearance or contact the administrators by 16 April 2026, a process that could see existing equity holders wiped out if the transfer is approved.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:BCB) stock is a Hold with a A$0.07 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Bowen Coking Coal Ltd stock, see the AU:BCB Stock Forecast page.

More about Bowen Coking Coal Ltd

Bowen Coking Coal Ltd is an Australian coal company focused on producing coking coal and related coal products through a group of entities including Bowen PCI, Coking Coal One, New Lenton Coal, Lenton Management and Marketing, and Bowen Coking Coal Marketing. The group is currently under financial distress and operating subject to a single deed of company arrangement overseen by voluntary administrators and receivers.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: A$8.08M

See more data about BCB stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Unity Metals Limited ( (AU:UM1) ) just unveiled an update.

Unity Metals Limited has scheduled its Annual General Meeting for Friday, 29 May 2026, with details on the venue and resolutions to be circulated to shareholders in a separate notice and published on the ASX platform and the company’s website. The meeting will include consideration of a director’s re-election, and the company has set 5:00 p.m. AWST on Monday, 13 April 2026 as the deadline for receiving written nominations from individuals wishing to stand for election to the board, providing clarity and structure to its upcoming governance process.

More about Unity Metals Limited

Unity Metals Limited is an ASX-listed gold and copper exploration company focused on Southeast Asia. It holds a sizeable ~700km² portfolio of highly prospective gold and copper-gold projects in Cambodia and Thailand, targeting intrusion-related gold and porphyry copper-gold deposits near established mining operations, including assets adjacent to Cambodia’s largest gold mine, Okvau.
